title: Stabilizer rank bounds for magic-state orbits
abstract: Distinct Clifford orbits of magic states can exhibit different stabilizer ranks at small tensor powers. We establish this for qutrits, where the single-qutrit Clifford group has four inequivalent orbits of magic states: Strange, Norrell, Hadamard-eigenstate, and the qutrit T-state, but a nontrivial upper bound on the asymptotic exponent had been pinned down for only the qutrit T-state. For the other three orbits we give explicit stabilizer decompositions, yielding upper bounds on the per-copy asymptotic stabilizer-rank exponent: $γ_S \le \log_3(2)/2 \approx 0.316$ for the Strange state, and $γ_{H_3}, γ_N \le \log_3(4)/3 \approx 0.421$ for the Hadamard-eigenstate and Norrell orbits, all strictly below the prior $γ_{T_3} \le 1/2$ baseline. We also prove the first nontrivial $Ω(m / \log m)$ asymptotic lower bounds for the Hadamard-eigenstate and Norrell orbits, and exhibit two-qutrit Clifford circuits that convert two copies of these states into an injectable phase state with constant success probability, enabling constant-overhead injection of one non-Clifford diagonal gate per orbit. In the case of qubits, we give a closed-form decomposition of the qubit T-type orbit at four copies matching the existing $γ_T \le \log_2(3)/4 \approx 0.396$ exponent via a direct algebraic identity rather than an entangled cat-state construction. An open-source library stabrank accompanies the paper, with Lean 4 proof formalizations of all the decompositions.
summary: This paper provides tighter bounds on the stabilizer rank of various magic-state orbits for both qubits and qutrits, which is crucial for understanding the complexity of simulating quantum circuits. It introduces a new software library and uses formal verification to ensure the accuracy of the discovered algebraic decompositions.
main_problem: Determining the stabilizer rank and the per-copy asymptotic stabilizer-rank exponent for different Clifford orbits of magic states in qubit and qutrit systems.
main_result: Established new, lower upper bounds for several qutrit magic-state orbits (Strange, Norrell, and Hadamard-eigenstate) and provided a closed-form qubit T-type decomposition. The authors also proved the first nontrivial asymptotic lower bounds for the Hadamard-eigenstate and Norrell orbits.
method: The authors use simulated-annealing searches via the 'stabrank' library to find stabilizer decompositions, apply a subset-sum argument for lower bounds, and utilize Lean 4 for formal machine-checked verification of the identities.
model_or_system: The study focuses on the Clifford + magic-state model of quantum computation, specifically analyzing the stabilizer rank of tensor powers of magic states in qubit (p=2) and qutrit (p=3) systems.
key_observables: Stabilizer rank (chi) and the per-copy asymptotic stabilizer-rank exponent (gamma).
important_parameters: Number of copies (m), dimension of the system (p=2 or p=3), and the specific Clifford orbits (Strange, Norrell, Hadamard-eigenstate, and T-state).
main_assumptions: The results assume the validity of the Clifford + magic-state model and rely on the algebraic structure of the Clifford group in prime dimensions.
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates the 'stabrank' decomposition pipeline, showing the flow from simulated annealing to numerical, symbolic, and formal verification.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of orbit-dependent stabilizer ranks, presents new upper bounds for qutrit orbits through explicit decompositions, establishes asymptotic lower bounds using subset-sum techniques, provides a new qubit T-type decomposition, and concludes with the introduction of the 'stabrank' software and formal verification results.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: Figure 1: The stabrank decomposition pipeline (upper-bound side). Simulated annealing returns a candidate canonical-form decomposition (k, x0, W, Q) with coefficients {ci}, replayed through three independent verifiers: numerical at machine precision (all seven qutrit identities), exact- rational in SymPy [MSP+17] (the heaviest identity, |N⟩⊗4), and Lean 4 + mathlib4 (all seven qutrit identities). The exhaustive k-tuple enumeration and the symplectic-quotient sweep (Section 3) instead emit JSON certificates audited via the path in Section 4.
